title: Recipes as Values
knowledge_note: In mathematics, functions can take other functions as input. Al-Khwarizmi used this concept in algebra 1200 years ago.
explanation: In ILMA, a recipe can be stored in a variable and passed around. A short recipe with no name is called a lambda.
starter_code:
remember double = recipe(n): give back n * 2
remember nums = bag[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
remember doubled = nums.map(double)
for each n in doubled:
    say n
worked_example:
remember double = recipe(n): give back n * 2
remember nums = bag[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
remember doubled = nums.map(double)
for each n in doubled:
    say n
challenge: Write a triple lambda and map it over the same bag.
hint: remember triple = recipe(n): give back n * 3
